exercises.title: KEM vs Key Exchange Comparison
exercises.description: Compare Key Encapsulation Mechanisms with traditional key exchange protocols.
tasks: Describe how Diffie-Hellman key exchange works
Describe how KEM-based key establishment works
Compare the number of messages required for each
Explain why KEMs are preferred for post-quantum cryptography
Identify scenarios where each approach is better suited
exercises.expectedOutput: A comparison document with protocol diagrams and analysis
hints: DH requires both parties to contribute randomness
KEM is non-interactive (sender generates the secret)

exercises.title: Parameter Set Selection Guide
exercises.description: Create a decision guide for selecting ML-KEM parameter sets.
tasks: List the key/ciphertext sizes for each parameter set
Define criteria for choosing each security level
Provide 3 example applications for each parameter set
Calculate bandwidth overhead compared to ECDH for TLS
Recommend a default parameter set with justification
exercises.expectedOutput: A parameter selection guide with examples and calculations
hints: ECDH public keys are ~32-65 bytes depending on curve
Consider regulatory requirements for each security level
Balance security margin against performance cost

exercises.title: LWE Problem Exploration
exercises.description: Research and explain the Learning With Errors problem.
tasks: Define the standard LWE problem mathematically
Explain how adding 'errors' makes the problem hard
Describe the difference between LWE, Ring-LWE, and Module-LWE
Explain why Module-LWE is used in ML-KEM
Discuss known attacks and their complexity
exercises.expectedOutput: A technical document explaining LWE for advanced readers
hints: Ring-LWE adds structure via polynomial rings
Module-LWE generalizes Ring-LWE with module structure

exercises.title: Hybrid Deployment Design
exercises.description: Design a hybrid key exchange protocol combining ECDH and ML-KEM.
tasks: Define the protocol flow with both algorithms
Specify how to combine the two shared secrets
Calculate the total bandwidth for the hybrid approach
Identify potential implementation pitfalls
Create test vectors for validation
exercises.expectedOutput: A protocol specification document with implementation guidance
hints: Use a KDF to combine secrets: K = KDF(ECDH_K || ML-KEM_K)
Both algorithms should use ephemeral keys
Consider ordering: which algorithm goes first?
